title: CSV imports bypass the route-level body-size guard when Content-Length is absent

evidence.quote:
// Post-read size check for requests without Content-Length header
    if (Buffer.byteLength(text, "utf8") > MAX_IMPORT_BODY_BYTES) {

reasoning: The route documents and implements a 4MB body cap, including a post-read check for requests without Content-Length. However, explicit text/csv requests are dispatched to handleCsvImport before the route reads the body and before the post-read byte check. If a chunked CSV upload omits Content-Length, this wrapper no longer enforces its advertised cap, unlike the JSON/text sniffing path.
reproduction: Send an authenticated text/csv request larger than 4MB without a Content-Length header. The route takes the early handleCsvImport branch instead of the MAX_IMPORT_BODY_BYTES post-read check in this file.
recommendation: Apply the same read-and-byte-check path to CSV requests before parsing, or make handleCsvImport accept a pre-read string/limit and enforce MAX_IMPORT_BODY_BYTES consistently for CSV content.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: CSV tests cover valid and malformed CSV parsing, but there is no oversized CSV test with absent Content-Length. The oversized checks are only implied by the JSON/text path comments in this file.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a text/csv request with Content-Length removed and a body larger than MAX_IMPORT_BODY_BYTES, then assert 413 and no importLabels/importSnapshotHashes writes.

history.note: Fixed by routing text/csv through the same read + 4MB byte guard before CSV parsing; added oversized no-Content-Length CSV regression.
